name: Reinsurance Broker Intro

subjectLines.a: New RVG program seeking placement — GPU Residual Value Guaranty
subjectLines.b: GPU RV insurance program — placement meeting request
subjectLines.c: Corgi: $3.465B ceded limit target — placement discussion

plainText:
Hi {{firstName}},

I'm reaching out from Corgi Insurance Services. We've built a Residual Value Guaranty (RVG) program for data-center GPU hardware and we're now building the reinsurance tower. We'd like to discuss placement.

The program in brief:

— Issuing carrier: DIAIC (Utah-domiciled captive), program manager: Corgi Insurance Services
— Product: put-style floor on GPU hardware value at a specified future date (3, 5, or 10 years)
— At expiry: if market value < guaranteed floor, policy pays full guaranteed amount; carrier takes title to hardware
— Policy tiers: 5%, 10%, 15% of original GPU value; premiums 2.1%–12.9%
— Loss basis: per-cohort occurrence — aggregation bounded by hardware cohort, not cross-site
— Pricing: SiliconNavigator™ daily benchmarks (80%+ H100/A100 market coverage) — independent, auditable
— Moral hazard: eliminated via mandatory title transfer at settlement and hardware recertification
— Actuarial basis: 20% annual depreciation assumption; Monte Carlo stress-tested across GPU generation cycles

Target ceded limit: $3.465B. We've raised $108M to capitalize the program. Primary buyers are GPU cloud operators with owned hardware (100–10,000 GPU clusters), with a secondary channel through lenders mandating RVG as a loan condition.

The GPU market is $100B today heading to $215B by 2030. This is a proven RV structure applied to a new — and very large — asset class. The reinsurance mechanics are the same as auto or equipment RV; the innovation is the pricing layer and the asset class.

{{#if specificDetail}}{{specificDetail}}

{{/if}}I'd welcome a 30-minute placement meeting to walk through the actuarial package, treaty structure, and how we're thinking about the tower build-out. Would this month work for your team?

{{callerName}}
Corgi Insurance Services
corgi.xyz

metadata.notes: Target: Guy Carpenter, Gallagher Re, Aon reinsurance practices. Lead with program size ($3.465B ceded limit target) and proven RV structure. Key objection: no GPU loss history — address with Monte Carlo model, SiliconNavigator pricing transparency, and title transfer structure. Always offer to share actuarial package as next step.
